_description: Michael's personal Bulger Peak completion queue with access season and ski mountaineering candidacy. climbed=true means done. best_season indicates the optimal window for a non-technical ascent. ski_mountaineering indicates whether the peak is a good ski descent objective.
_source: Manual dump from user 2026-04-20, enriched with access/ski data

peaks.name: Mount Rainier
peaks.notes: Classic ski descents: Fuhrer Finger, Ingraham Direct, Emmons Glacier. Corn season Apr–Jun.

peaks.name: Mount Adams
peaks.notes: South Spur is one of the best volcano ski descents in WA. Corn season Apr–Jun. SW Chutes for steeper skiing.

peaks.name: Little Tahoma
peaks.notes: Technical rock/ice summit block. Approach via Whitman Glacier from Summerland. Not a ski objective.

peaks.name: Mount Baker
peaks.notes: Excellent ski mountaineering via Coleman-Deming, Easton Glacier, or North Ridge. Long corn season.

peaks.name: Glacier Peak
peaks.notes: Remote but skiable via Sitkum or Cool glaciers. Long approach regardless of route.

peaks.name: Bonanza Peak
peaks.notes: Highest non-volcanic peak in WA. Approach via Holden Village or Railroad Creek. Glaciated; Mary Green Glacier route is standard. Not a ski objective—too technical.

peaks.name: Mount Stuart
peaks.notes: Cascadian Couloir is a classic spring ski line (Apr–Jun). Summer scramble via Longs Pass or Ingalls Creek.

peaks.name: Mount Fernow
peaks.notes: Remote approach via Phelps Creek + Leroy Creek. Often combined with 7FJ. Brushy lower approach. Road gate adds ~3 mi in spring.

peaks.name: Goode Mountain
peaks.notes: Technical NE Buttress is the standard route. Approach via North Fork Bridge Creek. Steep and exposed.

peaks.name: Mount Shuksan
peaks.notes: Sulphide Glacier is a classic ski descent. Fisher Chimneys for summer scramble. Shannon Ridge approach opens early.

peaks.name: Mount Buckner
peaks.notes: Approach via Cascade Pass + Horseshoe Basin. North Face has snow but not a typical ski objective. Requires Cascade River Rd access.

peaks.name: Seven Fingered Jack
peaks.notes: Approach via Phelps Creek to Spider Meadow. Often combined with Maude/Fernow. Road gate adds ~3 mi in spring.

peaks.name: Mount Logan
peaks.notes: Remote. Typically approached via Thunder Creek or Douglas Glacier. Often combined with other Skagit peaks. Long day or bivy.

peaks.name: Jack Mountain
peaks.notes: Very remote. Approach via Nohokomeen Glacier or Devils Creek. SR 20 must be open. One of the most remote Bulgers.

peaks.name: Mount Maude
peaks.notes: Approach via Phelps Creek to Ice Lakes basin. Non-technical scramble from Ice Lakes. Often combined with 7FJ. Road gate adds ~3 mi in spring.

peaks.name: Mount Spickard
peaks.notes: Extremely remote. Approach via Depot Creek or Chilliwack River (Canada). Multi-day trip mandatory. Often combined with Redoubt and Custer.

peaks.name: Black Peak
peaks.notes: Approach via SR 20 / Wing Lake. Requires SR 20 open. Class 3 scramble. Not a ski objective.

peaks.name: Mount Redoubt
peaks.notes: Very remote. Usually combined with Spickard. Multi-day trip via Depot Creek or Chilliwack approach.

peaks.name: Copper Peak
peaks.notes: Often combined with Fernow. Approach via Phelps Creek. Requires crossing Copper/Fernow ridge.

peaks.name: North Gardner Mountain
peaks.notes: Approach via Wolf Creek from Methow Valley. Long day—often combined with Gardner. Trail melts out earlier than most due to east-side aspect. Non-technical scramble.

peaks.name: Dome Peak
peaks.notes: One of the most remote Bulgers. Approach via Downey Creek or Bachelor Creek. Glaciated. Multi-day trip or very long day.

peaks.name: Gardner Mountain
peaks.notes: Approach via Wolf Creek. Usually combined with N Gardner. East-side peak—snow melts early. Non-technical scramble.

peaks.name: Boston Peak
peaks.notes: Approach via Cascade Pass to Boston Basin. Requires Cascade River Rd access. Glaciated. Often combined with Sahale/Forbidden.

peaks.name: Silver Star Mountain
peaks.notes: Via Silver Star Creek or Cedar Creek. East-side aspect, melts out early. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Eldorado Peak
peaks.notes: Eldorado Glacier provides a ski descent option. Approach via Cascade River Rd / Eldorado TH. Road opens early in season.

peaks.name: Dragontail Peak
peaks.notes: Via Colchuck Lake and Aasgard Pass. Steep scramble. Couloir has snow into early summer but not a classic ski line.

peaks.name: Forbidden Peak
peaks.notes: Technical alpine climb. West Ridge is the standard route (class 4). Approach via Boston Basin from Cascade River Rd.

peaks.name: Mesahchie Peak
peaks.notes: Remote. Approach via Fisher Creek or Thunder Creek. Requires SR 20. Often a multi-day objective.

peaks.name: Oval Peak
peaks.notes: East-side Methow peak. Long approach via Oval Creek or Eagle Creek. Non-technical scramble.

peaks.name: Fortress Mountain
peaks.notes: Approach via Phelps Creek or Chiwawa River. Remote but non-technical.

peaks.name: Mount Lago
peaks.notes: East-side Pasayten peak. Via Horseshoe Basin or PCT approach. Melts out early. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Robinson Mountain
peaks.notes: East-side Methow/Pasayten. Non-technical walk-up once snow clears.

peaks.name: Colchuck Peak
peaks.notes: Approach via Colchuck Lake. Class 3 scramble. Often combined with Dragontail.

peaks.name: Star Peak
peaks.notes: Remote central Cascades. Non-technical but long approach.

peaks.name: Remmel Mountain
peaks.notes: Eastern Pasayten. Long approach via Andrews Creek or Tungsten Mine trail. Dry east-side peak. Walk-up once snow melts. Very long day trip from North Bend.

peaks.name: Katsuk Peak
peaks.notes: Remote. Near Kimtah/Cosho. Approach via Fisher Creek or Thunder Creek. Requires SR 20.

peaks.name: Sahale Peak
peaks.notes: Sahale Glacier provides ski option. Approach via Cascade Pass. One of the more accessible N Cascades glaciated peaks.

peaks.name: Cannon Mountain
peaks.notes: Via Phelps Creek. Non-technical. Often combined with other Entiat peaks.

peaks.name: Mount Custer
peaks.notes: Very remote. Usually combined with Spickard/Redoubt on a multi-day trip via Depot Creek.

peaks.name: Ptarmigan Peak
peaks.notes: East-side Pasayten. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Sherpa Peak
peaks.notes: Stuart Range. Approach via Ingalls Creek or Colchuck Lake. Class 3.

peaks.name: Clark Mountain
peaks.notes: Remote. Approach via Phelps Creek or Clark Creek. Long approach. Often combined with other Entiat peaks.

peaks.name: Cathedral Peak
peaks.notes: Eastern Pasayten. Approach via Andrews Creek or Cathedral Lakes. Non-technical but very long day from North Bend.

peaks.name: Kimtah Peak
peaks.notes: Remote. Near Katsuk/Cosho cluster. Approach via Fisher Creek. Requires SR 20.

peaks.name: Mount Carru
peaks.notes: East-side Okanogan. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Monument Peak
peaks.notes: East-side Okanogan. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Cardinal Peak
peaks.notes: Remote Chelan Mountains. Approach via Emerald Park or Railroad Creek. Long approach. Often combined with Emerald/Saska.

peaks.name: Osceola Peak
peaks.notes: East-side Okanogan. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Raven Ridge
peaks.notes: Remote. Approach from Chilliwack or via Depot Creek. Usually combined with Spickard/Redoubt/Custer multi-day trip.

peaks.name: Buck Mountain
peaks.notes: Via Chiwawa River / Buck Creek Pass approach. Non-technical. Long day trip.

peaks.name: Storm King
peaks.notes: Near Goode Mountain. Approach via North Fork Bridge Creek. Technical scramble. Remote.

peaks.name: Enchantment Peak
peaks.notes: Stuart Range via Aasgard Pass. Class 3 scramble.

peaks.name: Reynolds Peak
peaks.notes: East-side Methow. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Martin Peak (N Cascades)
peaks.notes: Remote Central N Cascades. Approach via Phelps Creek or Leroy Creek. Not to be confused with Martin Peak (Methow).

peaks.name: Primus Peak
peaks.notes: Remote. Often combined with other Central N Cascades peaks. Approach via Downey Creek or similar.

peaks.name: Dark Peak
peaks.notes: Very remote. Near Dome Peak. Multi-day trip. Low prominence—often a tag on a bigger trip.

peaks.name: Mox Peaks
peaks.notes: Very remote Skagit Range. Technical twin summits. Multi-day approach. One of the hardest Bulgers.

peaks.name: Cashmere Mountain
peaks.notes: Wenatchee Mountains. Non-technical scramble.

peaks.name: Klawatti Peak
peaks.notes: Approach via Cascade River Rd / Boston Basin area. Glaciated.

peaks.name: Horseshoe Peak
peaks.notes: Very low prominence—essentially a subpeak. Near Buckner. Approach via Horseshoe Basin from Cascade Pass.

peaks.name: Mount Rahm
peaks.notes: Very remote Skagit Range. Near Spickard/Custer. Multi-day trip.

peaks.name: Big Craggy Peak
peaks.notes: East-side Pasayten. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Hoodoo Peak
peaks.notes: Methow Mountains. Near Gardner/Bigelow. Non-technical scramble. Can combine with nearby peaks.

peaks.name: Lost Peak
peaks.notes: East-side Okanogan. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Chiwawa Mountain
peaks.notes: Via Phelps Creek. Non-technical. Long approach.

peaks.name: Argonaut Peak
peaks.notes: Stuart Range. Class 3-4 scramble. Approach via Colchuck or Ingalls Creek.

peaks.name: Tower Mountain
peaks.notes: Eastern Pasayten. Very long approach from any direction. Non-technical once there. One of the most remote east-side peaks.

peaks.name: Little Annapurna
peaks.notes: Stuart Range via Aasgard Pass. Walk-up from upper Enchantments.

peaks.name: Mount Bigelow
peaks.notes: Methow Mountains. Near Gardner/Hoodoo cluster. Non-technical. Combine with Gardner trip.

peaks.name: Dorado Needle
peaks.notes: Near Eldorado. Class 4 summit block. Approach via Cascade River Rd.

peaks.name: Sinister Peak
peaks.notes: Very remote. Near Dome Peak. Multi-day approach via Downey Creek or Agnes Creek.

peaks.name: Emerald Peak
peaks.notes: Remote Chelan Mountains. Often combined with Cardinal/Saska. Long approach via Railroad Creek.

peaks.name: Dumbell Mountain
peaks.notes: Entiat Mountains. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Greenwood Mountain
peaks.notes: Entiat Mountains. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Mox Peaks-Northwest Peak
peaks.notes: NW summit of Twin Spires. Same remote approach as Mox Peaks SE. Multi-day.

peaks.name: Saska Peak
peaks.notes: Remote Chelan Mountains. Often combined with Cardinal/Emerald. Long approach.

peaks.name: Luahna Peak
peaks.notes: Near Clark/Fortress cluster. Approach via Phelps Creek. Often combined with other Entiat peaks.

peaks.name: Pinnacle Mountain
peaks.notes: Remote Chelan Mountains. Long approach. Often combined with Cardinal/Emerald/Saska.

peaks.name: Azurite Peak
peaks.notes: Approach via Chancellor trail or PCT. Requires SR 20 for most approaches. Non-technical scramble.

peaks.name: Blackcap Mountain
peaks.notes: East-side Okanogan. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Courtney Peak
peaks.notes: Methow Mountains. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Spectacle Buttes
peaks.notes: Entiat Mountains. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Martin Peak (Methow)
peaks.notes: East-side Methow. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Lake Mountain
peaks.notes: East-side Okanogan. Non-technical.

peaks.name: West Craggy
peaks.notes: East-side Okanogan. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Golden Horn
peaks.notes: Approach via PCT/Methow Pass or Swamp Creek. Requires SR 20 for shortest approach. Dramatic spire—class 3 scramble.

peaks.name: McClellan Peak
peaks.notes: Stuart Range. Non-technical scramble.

peaks.name: Devore Peak
peaks.notes: Near Chiwawa/Luahna cluster. Approach via Phelps Creek. Non-technical but remote.

peaks.name: Amphitheater Mountain
peaks.notes: Eastern Pasayten. Non-technical walk-up. Very long drive from North Bend but dry east-side access.

peaks.name: Snowfield Peak
peaks.notes: Approach via Neve Glacier from Colonial Creek. Requires SR 20.

peaks.name: Austera Peak
peaks.notes: Near Klawatti/Eldorado. Approach from Cascade River Rd via Eldorado Glacier area. Low prominence—tag on an Eldorado trip.

peaks.name: Windy Peak
peaks.notes: Eastern Pasayten. Non-technical. Very long drive. Walk-up once snow melts.

peaks.name: Mount Saint Helens
peaks.notes: Permit required. Monitor Ridge is standard. Excellent spring ski descent—Worm Flows, Swift Glacier, or Monitor Ridge. Corn season Mar–Jun.

peaks.name: Cosho Peak
peaks.notes: Remote. Near Kimtah/Katsuk cluster. Approach via Fisher Creek. Requires SR 20.

peaks.name: Big Snagtooth
peaks.notes: Remote Methow. Technical summit—one of the harder Bulger scrambles. Class 4+.

peaks.name: Mount Formidable
peaks.notes: Approach via Cascade Pass or Downey Creek. Glaciated. Can be combined with Spider/Sinister. Remote.

peaks.name: Switchback Mountain
peaks.notes: Methow Mountains. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Abernathy Peak
peaks.notes: Methow Mountains. Non-technical.

peaks.name: Tupshin Peak
peaks.notes: Remote. Near Devore/Primus. Approach via Phelps Creek or Entiat River. Non-technical but very long.

peaks.name: Flora Mountain
peaks.notes: Remote Central N Cascades. Approach via Downey Creek or Agnes Creek. Non-technical but multi-day.
